title: limonium minoricense
intro: Limonium minoricense, also known as Sea Lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, blue-purple flowers and dense clusters. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits associated with Sea Lavender include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils that can be used topically or ingested for their therapeutic benefits.
